conditions.name: Viral Fever
conditions.description: A common viral infection causing elevated body temperature and general discomfort.

conditions.name: Bacterial Infection
conditions.description: An infection caused by bacteria that may affect various body parts.

conditions.name: Common Cold
conditions.description: A mild viral respiratory infection affecting the nose and throat.

conditions.name: Influenza (Flu)
conditions.description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses.

conditions.name: Migraine
conditions.description: A neurological condition causing severe, recurring headaches often with nausea.

conditions.name: Tension Headache
conditions.description: The most common type of headache caused by stress or muscle tension.

conditions.name: Sinusitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the sinuses, often due to infection or allergy.

conditions.name: Allergic Rhinitis
conditions.description: An allergic response causing cold-like symptoms triggered by allergens.

conditions.name: Bronchitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes causing cough and mucus production.

conditions.name: Asthma
conditions.description: A chronic respiratory condition causing airway narrowing and breathing difficulty.

conditions.name: Pneumonia
conditions.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs.

conditions.name: Hypertension
conditions.description: Persistently high blood pressure that increases the risk of heart disease.

conditions.name: Hypotension
conditions.description: Abnormally low blood pressure causing dizziness and fainting.

conditions.name: Gastroenteritis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically caused by infection.

conditions.name: Acid Reflux / GERD
conditions.description: A condition where stomach acid frequently flows back into the esophagus.

conditions.name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
conditions.description: A chronic gastrointestinal disorder affecting the large intestine.

conditions.name: Urinary Tract Infection
conditions.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system caused by bacteria.

conditions.name: Kidney Stones
conditions.description: Hard deposits of minerals and salts that form inside the kidneys.

conditions.name: Conjunctivitis (Pink Eye)
conditions.description: Inflammation or infection of the transparent membrane lining the eyelid.

conditions.name: Otitis Media (Ear Infection)
conditions.description: An infection of the middle ear, most common in children.

conditions.name: Type 2 Diabetes
conditions.description: A chronic metabolic condition where blood sugar levels remain too high.

conditions.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions.description: A condition where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough thyroid hormone.

conditions.name: Hyperthyroidism
conditions.description: A condition where the thyroid produces too much thyroid hormone.

conditions.name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
conditions.description: A blood disorder caused by insufficient iron, resulting in fewer red blood cells.

conditions.name: Anxiety Disorder
conditions.description: A mental health condition characterized by persistent, excessive worry.

conditions.name: Depression
conditions.description: A mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest.

conditions.name: Osteoarthritis
conditions.description: Degeneration of joint cartilage, causing pain and stiffness.

conditions.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
conditions.description: An autoimmune disorder that primarily affects joints causing inflammation.

conditions.name: Eczema
conditions.description: A condition making the skin red, inflamed, and itchy.

conditions.name: Psoriasis
conditions.description: An autoimmune disease causing rapid skin cell growth and scaling patches.

conditions.name: Dengue Fever
conditions.description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing severe flu-like illness.

conditions.name: Malaria
conditions.description: A life-threatening disease transmitted by infected mosquitoes.

conditions.name: Typhoid Fever
conditions.description: A bacterial infection spread through contaminated food and water.

conditions.name: Peptic Ulcer
conditions.description: Open sores on the inner lining of the stomach or upper small intestine.

conditions.name: Gallstones
conditions.description: Hardened deposits in the gallbladder that can cause abdominal pain.

conditions.name: Hepatitis A
conditions.description: A viral liver infection spread through contaminated food and water.

conditions.name: Hepatitis B
conditions.description: A serious liver infection caused by the hepatitis B virus.

conditions.name: Chickenpox
conditions.description: A highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy blister-like rash.

conditions.name: Scabies
conditions.description: A contagious skin infestation caused by tiny mites burrowing under the skin.

conditions.name: Fungal Infection (Skin)
conditions.description: Skin infections caused by various types of fungi, e.g., ringworm.

conditions.name: COVID-19
conditions.description: A respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.

conditions.name: Appendicitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the appendix causing acute abdominal pain.

conditions.name: Haemorrhoids (Piles)
conditions.description: Swollen veins in the lower rectum causing discomfort and bleeding.

conditions.name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
conditions.description: A hormonal disorder causing irregular periods and polycystic ovaries.

conditions.name: Dysmenorrhea
conditions.description: Painful menstruation causing cramping in the lower abdomen.

conditions.name: Lower Back Pain (Muscular)
conditions.description: Pain in the lower back due to muscle strain or poor posture.

conditions.name: Cervical Spondylosis
conditions.description: Age-related wear of the spinal discs in the neck.

conditions.name: Peripheral Neuropathy
conditions.description: Damage to the peripheral nerves causing weakness, numbness, and pain.

conditions.name: Insomnia
conditions.description: A sleep disorder causing difficulty falling or staying asleep.

conditions.name: Panic Disorder
conditions.description: A type of anxiety disorder characterized by recurring panic attacks.

conditions.name: Vitamin D Deficiency
conditions.description: A common condition where the body lacks adequate Vitamin D.

conditions.name: Vitamin B12 Deficiency
conditions.description: Low B12 levels causing neurological and blood-related symptoms.

conditions.name: Dehydration
conditions.description: A condition where the body loses more fluids than it takes in.

conditions.name: Heat Stroke
conditions.description: A serious condition where the body overheats due to prolonged exposure to high temperatures.

conditions.name: Tonsillitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the tonsils, usually due to viral or bacterial infection.

conditions.name: Dental Cavity
conditions.description: Permanently damaged areas in the hard surface of teeth.

conditions.name: Gingivitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the gums due to plaque build-up.

conditions.name: Varicose Veins
conditions.description: Enlarged, twisted veins, commonly in the legs.

medicines.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
medicines.use: Reduces fever and relieves mild to moderate pain
medicines.precautions: Avoid alcohol; do not exceed recommended dose; caution in liver disease

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.use: Anti-inflammatory, relieves pain, reduces fever
medicines.precautions: Avoid on empty stomach; caution in kidney/stomach issues

medicines.name: Aspirin
medicines.use: Pain relief, fever reduction, blood thinning
medicines.dosage_guidance: 300–600mg every 4 hours; low-dose 75–100mg for cardiac use
medicines.precautions: Not for children under 16; avoid with blood thinners; avoid in bleeding disorders

medicines.name: Amoxicillin
medicines.use: Treats bacterial infections of the ear, throat, chest, and urinary tract
medicines.dosage_guidance: 250–500mg three times daily; complete full course
medicines.precautions: Check for penicillin allergy; complete the course

medicines.name: Cetirizine
medicines.use: Relieves symptoms of allergies, hay fever, hives
medicines.precautions: May cause drowsiness; avoid alcohol

medicines.name: Loratadine
medicines.use: Non-drowsy antihistamine for allergies and hives
medicines.precautions: Fewer sedation effects than older antihistamines

medicines.name: Omeprazole
medicines.use: Reduces stomach acid for heartburn, GERD, ulcers
medicines.precautions: Long-term use may affect B12 and magnesium levels

medicines.name: Pantoprazole
medicines.use: Treats acid reflux, GERD, and peptic ulcers
medicines.precautions: Not for long-term use without medical supervision

medicines.name: Ranitidine
medicines.use: Reduces stomach acid in heartburn and ulcers
medicines.precautions: Consult doctor for prolonged use; note: some brands recalled in some countries

medicines.name: Metformin
medicines.use: Controls blood sugar in Type 2 Diabetes
medicines.dosage_guidance: 500mg once or twice daily with meals; increase gradually
medicines.precautions: Monitor kidney function; take with food to reduce nausea

medicines.name: Amlodipine
medicines.use: Treats high blood pressure and chest pain (angina)
medicines.precautions: May cause ankle swelling; do not stop suddenly

medicines.name: Atenolol
medicines.use: Treats high blood pressure and heart rhythm problems
medicines.precautions: Do not stop abruptly; caution in asthma patients

medicines.name: Lisinopril
medicines.use: Treats high blood pressure and heart failure
medicines.precautions: May cause dry cough; check potassium levels regularly

medicines.name: Metronidazole
medicines.use: Treats bacterial and parasitic infections
medicines.precautions: Avoid alcohol during treatment and 48 hrs after

medicines.name: Azithromycin
medicines.use: Treats respiratory and skin infections
medicines.precautions: Take on empty stomach; watch for heart rhythm issues

medicines.name: Ciprofloxacin
medicines.use: Treats UTI, respiratory, and gastrointestinal infections
medicines.precautions: Drink plenty of water; avoid antacids within 2 hrs

medicines.name: Levothyroxine
medicines.use: Replaces thyroid hormone in hypothyroidism
medicines.dosage_guidance: Take on empty stomach 30 min before breakfast
medicines.precautions: Do not adjust dose without doctor approval; avoid calcium/iron supplements at same time

medicines.name: Salbutamol (Albuterol) Inhaler
medicines.use: Relieves asthma and breathing difficulty
medicines.dosage_guidance: 1–2 puffs as needed; not more than 4 times/day
medicines.precautions: Overuse can reduce effectiveness; use spacer for children

medicines.name: Montelukast
medicines.use: Prevents asthma attacks and relieves allergy symptoms
medicines.precautions: May affect mood and behaviour; report any mental health changes

medicines.name: Hydroxychloroquine
medicines.use: Treats malaria and rheumatoid arthritis
medicines.dosage_guidance: As directed by doctor; not for self-use
medicines.precautions: Regular eye checks needed for long-term use

medicines.name: Chloroquine
medicines.use: Prevents and treats malaria
medicines.precautions: Not used in regions with resistant strains; regular eye monitoring

medicines.name: Artemether + Lumefantrine
medicines.use: First-line treatment for uncomplicated malaria
medicines.dosage_guidance: 6 doses over 3 days; take with food
medicines.precautions: Complete the full course; do not use in first trimester

medicines.name: Domperidone
medicines.use: Relieves nausea, vomiting, and bloating
medicines.dosage_guidance: 10mg up to three times daily before meals
medicines.precautions: Not for long-term use; caution in heart conditions

medicines.name: Ondansetron
medicines.use: Prevents and treats nausea and vomiting
medicines.precautions: May affect heart rhythm; use minimum effective dose

medicines.name: ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts)
medicines.use: Rehydration during diarrhea, vomiting, and fever
medicines.dosage_guidance: Dissolve one sachet in 1 litre of clean water; drink as needed
medicines.precautions: Not a substitute for medical treatment in severe dehydration

medicines.name: Loperamide
medicines.use: Reduces diarrhea by slowing bowel movement
medicines.dosage_guidance: 4mg initially, then 2mg after each loose stool; max 16mg/day
medicines.precautions: Not for children under 2; stop if symptoms worsen after 48 hrs

medicines.name: Bisacodyl
medicines.use: Relieves occasional constipation
medicines.dosage_guidance: 5–10mg at night; effect usually in 6–12 hours
medicines.precautions: Not for prolonged use; ensure adequate fluid intake

medicines.name: Lactulose
medicines.use: Treats constipation and liver-related conditions
medicines.dosage_guidance: 15–30ml once or twice daily; adjust for effect
medicines.precautions: May cause bloating initially; drink plenty of fluids

medicines.name: Antacid (Aluminium Hydroxide + Magnesium Hydroxide)
medicines.use: Neutralises stomach acid for heartburn and indigestion
medicines.dosage_guidance: 1–2 tablets or 10ml after meals and at bedtime
medicines.precautions: Do not take within 2 hours of other medications

medicines.name: Simethicone
medicines.use: Relieves gas and bloating
medicines.precautions: Generally safe; not absorbed systemically

medicines.name: Diclofenac
medicines.use: Relieves pain and inflammation in joints and muscles
medicines.dosage_guidance: 25–50mg two to three times daily with food
medicines.precautions: Avoid in kidney/heart conditions; take with food

medicines.name: Naproxen
medicines.use: Treats pain, inflammation, and fever
medicines.precautions: Take with food; not for long-term use without supervision

medicines.name: Tramadol
medicines.use: Moderate to severe pain relief
medicines.precautions: Risk of dependence; avoid alcohol; not for children

medicines.name: Prednisolone
medicines.use: Reduces inflammation in allergic and autoimmune conditions
medicines.precautions: Do not stop abruptly; monitor blood sugar and bone density

medicines.name: Hydrocortisone Cream
medicines.use: Treats skin inflammation, eczema, and allergic rashes
medicines.precautions: Avoid on face and broken skin; not for long-term use

medicines.name: Clotrimazole Cream
medicines.use: Treats fungal skin infections like ringworm and athlete's foot
medicines.precautions: Continue use for full duration even if symptoms improve

medicines.name: Fluconazole
medicines.use: Treats yeast infections and fungal diseases
medicines.dosage_guidance: 150mg single dose for yeast infection; other infections as directed
medicines.precautions: Check for drug interactions; caution in liver disease

medicines.name: Acyclovir
medicines.use: Treats herpes simplex, chickenpox, and shingles
medicines.precautions: Stay well hydrated; start early in infection for best effect

medicines.name: Iron Supplement (Ferrous Sulphate)
medicines.use: Treats iron deficiency anemia
medicines.dosage_guidance: 200mg 2–3 times daily; take with Vitamin C for better absorption
medicines.precautions: May cause constipation and dark stools; take on empty stomach if tolerated

medicines.name: Folic Acid
medicines.use: Treats and prevents folate deficiency; essential in pregnancy
medicines.precautions: Essential for pregnant women; high doses need medical guidance

medicines.name: Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol)
medicines.use: Treats Vitamin D deficiency; supports bone health
medicines.dosage_guidance: 600–2000 IU daily; higher doses only as prescribed
medicines.precautions: Avoid high-dose supplementation without blood test

medicines.name: Vitamin B12 (Cyanocobalamin)
medicines.use: Treats B12 deficiency and nerve-related symptoms
medicines.precautions: Injections may be needed if absorption is poor

medicines.name: Calcium + Vitamin D3
medicines.use: Supports bone health; prevents osteoporosis
medicines.dosage_guidance: 500–1000mg Calcium with 400 IU D3 twice daily
medicines.precautions: Avoid excess calcium; separate from iron supplements

medicines.name: Zinc Supplement
medicines.use: Supports immunity, wound healing, and diarrhea treatment
medicines.precautions: Excessive zinc can interfere with copper absorption

medicines.name: Doxycycline
medicines.use: Treats bacterial infections and malaria prophylaxis
medicines.dosage_guidance: 100mg twice daily; take with food and water
medicines.precautions: Avoid sunlight exposure; not for children under 8 or pregnant women

medicines.name: Cephalexin
medicines.use: Treats skin, throat, and urinary tract bacterial infections
medicines.precautions: Complete full course; caution in penicillin allergy

medicines.name: Cotrimoxazole (Trimethoprim + Sulfamethoxazole)
medicines.use: Treats UTI, respiratory, and ear infections
medicines.dosage_guidance: One double-strength tablet twice daily for 5–10 days
medicines.precautions: Drink plenty of water; allergic reactions possible

medicines.name: Atorvastatin
medicines.use: Lowers cholesterol and reduces cardiovascular risk
medicines.precautions: Monitor liver enzymes; report muscle pain

medicines.name: Rosuvastatin
medicines.use: Reduces bad cholesterol (LDL) and triglycerides
medicines.precautions: Avoid grapefruit; monitor for muscle weakness

medicines.name: Warfarin
medicines.use: Blood thinner for preventing clots in heart conditions
medicines.dosage_guidance: Dose adjusted based on INR levels; as prescribed
medicines.precautions: Regular INR monitoring required; many food/drug interactions

medicines.name: Clopidogrel
medicines.use: Prevents blood clots after heart attack or stroke
medicines.precautions: Increased bleeding risk; avoid NSAIDs without doctor advice

medicines.name: Sertraline
medicines.use: Treats depression, anxiety, OCD, and PTSD
medicines.dosage_guidance: 50–200mg once daily; takes 4–6 weeks to show full effect
medicines.precautions: Do not stop abruptly; monitor for mood changes initially

medicines.name: Escitalopram
medicines.use: Treats depression and generalised anxiety disorder
medicines.precautions: May cause initial anxiety; do not stop suddenly

medicines.name: Alprazolam
medicines.use: Short-term relief of anxiety and panic disorders
medicines.precautions: Risk of dependence; avoid alcohol; not for long-term use

medicines.name: Melatonin
medicines.use: Helps regulate sleep-wake cycle for insomnia
medicines.precautions: Short-term use recommended; may cause drowsiness

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine
medicines.use: Antihistamine that also aids sleep and motion sickness
medicines.precautions: Causes significant drowsiness; not for long-term sleep use

medicines.name: Promethazine
medicines.use: Treats allergies, nausea, and motion sickness
medicines.precautions: Causes sedation; avoid driving; not for children under 2

medicines.name: Betahistine
medicines.use: Treats vertigo and Meniere's disease
medicines.precautions: Caution in asthma and peptic ulcer

medicines.name: Prochlorperazine
medicines.use: Treats nausea, vomiting, and vertigo
medicines.precautions: May cause drowsiness and movement side effects

medicines.name: Dexamethasone
medicines.use: Treats severe inflammation and allergic conditions
medicines.dosage_guidance: 0.5–10mg daily; taper as per doctor's advice
medicines.precautions: Do not stop abruptly; raises blood sugar; immune suppression

medicines.name: Mometasone Nasal Spray
medicines.use: Reduces nasal congestion and inflammation in allergic rhinitis
medicines.dosage_guidance: 2 sprays in each nostril once daily
medicines.precautions: For nasal use only; rinse mouth after use

medicines.name: Xylometazoline Nasal Drops
medicines.use: Relieves nasal congestion
medicines.dosage_guidance: 2–3 drops in each nostril every 8–10 hours; max 3 days
medicines.precautions: Do not use more than 3 days; rebound congestion may occur

medicines.name: Benzydamine Gargle / Spray
medicines.use: Relieves sore throat and mouth inflammation
medicines.precautions: Not for children under 6; do not swallow

medicines.name: Chlorhexidine Mouthwash
medicines.use: Prevents and treats gum disease and oral infections
medicines.dosage_guidance: Rinse with 10–15ml for 30 seconds twice daily
medicines.precautions: May stain teeth with prolonged use; do not swallow

medicines.name: Povidone-Iodine (Betadine)
medicines.use: Antiseptic for wound cleaning and skin infections
medicines.dosage_guidance: Apply diluted solution to affected area 1–2 times daily
medicines.precautions: Not for large wounds; avoid if thyroid issues present

medicines.name: Silver Sulfadiazine Cream
medicines.use: Prevents and treats infection in burns and wounds
medicines.dosage_guidance: Apply 1–2 times daily to affected area
medicines.precautions: Avoid in pregnancy; monitor for allergic reaction

medicines.name: Mupirocin Cream
medicines.use: Treats bacterial skin infections like impetigo
medicines.precautions: For external use only; avoid eyes

medicines.name: Permethrin Cream
medicines.use: Treats scabies and head lice
medicines.dosage_guidance: Apply full body below neck; wash off after 8–14 hours
medicines.precautions: Avoid eyes and broken skin; repeat in 1 week if needed

medicines.name: Albendazole
medicines.use: Treats intestinal worm infections
medicines.precautions: Avoid in pregnancy; take with fatty meal for best absorption

medicines.name: Mebendazole
medicines.use: Treats threadworm, roundworm, and hookworm infections
medicines.dosage_guidance: 100mg twice daily for 3 days or 500mg single dose
medicines.precautions: Treat all household members; ensure hand hygiene

medicines.name: Colchicine
medicines.use: Treats and prevents gout attacks
medicines.dosage_guidance: 0.5–1mg daily or as directed for acute attack
medicines.precautions: Do not exceed recommended dose; may cause diarrhea

medicines.name: Allopurinol
medicines.use: Reduces uric acid levels to prevent gout
medicines.precautions: Start after acute attack subsides; drink plenty of water

medicines.name: Furosemide
medicines.use: Reduces fluid retention in heart failure and hypertension
medicines.precautions: Monitor electrolytes; avoid at night

medicines.name: Spironolactone
medicines.use: Treats fluid retention and heart failure; used in PCOS
medicines.precautions: Monitor potassium levels; avoid in kidney failure

medicines.name: Glibenclamide
medicines.use: Lowers blood sugar in Type 2 Diabetes
medicines.precautions: Risk of hypoglycemia; avoid skipping meals

medicines.name: Insulin (Regular)
medicines.use: Controls blood glucose in diabetes (Type 1 and Type 2)
medicines.precautions: Monitor blood sugar frequently; proper injection technique required

medicines.name: Methylprednisolone
medicines.use: Reduces severe inflammation in autoimmune and allergic conditions
medicines.dosage_guidance: As prescribed; available as tablet and injection
medicines.precautions: Short courses preferred; taper gradually

medicines.name: Clobetasol Cream
medicines.use: Treats severe skin inflammation, psoriasis, and eczema
medicines.dosage_guidance: Apply thin layer once or twice daily for up to 2 weeks
medicines.precautions: Potent steroid; do not use on face or for extended periods

medicines.name: Calamine Lotion
medicines.use: Soothes itching from insect bites, rashes, and chickenpox
medicines.dosage_guidance: Apply to affected area as needed
medicines.precautions: External use only; avoid eyes; reapply as needed

medicines.name: Minoxidil (Topical)
medicines.use: Treats hair loss and stimulates hair regrowth
medicines.dosage_guidance: Apply 1ml twice daily to scalp; takes 3–6 months to see results
medicines.precautions: Do not use on broken skin; wash hands after application

medicines.name: Eye Drops (Lubricating / Artificial Tears)
medicines.use: Relieves dry, irritated eyes
medicines.dosage_guidance: 1–2 drops in each eye as needed
medicines.precautions: Discard 28 days after opening; avoid touching dropper tip to eye

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops
medicines.use: Treats bacterial eye infections
medicines.precautions: Complete full course; do not share eye drops

medicines.name: Ear Drops (Otosporin / Sofradex)
medicines.use: Treats ear infections and inflammation
medicines.dosage_guidance: 3–4 drops in affected ear 3–4 times daily
medicines.precautions: Warm drops to body temperature before use; do not use if eardrum is perforated

medicines.name: Nystatin
medicines.use: Treats oral and intestinal candida (yeast) infections
medicines.dosage_guidance: 100,000–500,000 units four times daily; swish and swallow
medicines.precautions: Complete full course; shake suspension well

medicines.name: Amlodipine + Atorvastatin Combination
medicines.use: Controls blood pressure and cholesterol simultaneously
medicines.dosage_guidance: As prescribed; once daily at night
medicines.precautions: Monitor liver and renal function; report muscle pain

medicines.name: Hydralazine
medicines.use: Treats high blood pressure
medicines.precautions: May cause rapid heart rate; often used in combination

medicines.name: Gabapentin
medicines.use: Treats nerve pain and seizures
medicines.precautions: Causes dizziness and sedation; do not stop abruptly

medicines.name: Pregabalin
medicines.use: Treats neuropathic pain, fibromyalgia, and anxiety
medicines.precautions: Risk of dizziness; do not stop abruptly; caution if operating machinery

medicines.name: Sucralfate
medicines.use: Treats peptic ulcers by coating the stomach lining
medicines.dosage_guidance: 1g four times daily on empty stomach
medicines.precautions: Separate from other medications by 2 hours

medicines.name: Mefenamic Acid
medicines.use: Treats period pain and mild to moderate pain
medicines.precautions: Not for more than 7 days; avoid in kidney disease

medicines.name: Tranexamic Acid
medicines.use: Reduces excessive menstrual bleeding and controls clotting
medicines.precautions: Not for use in thromboembolic conditions

medicines.name: Drotaverine
medicines.use: Relieves smooth muscle spasms in abdominal and pelvic pain
medicines.precautions: Avoid in severe heart/liver/kidney disease

medicines.name: Hyoscine Butylbromide
medicines.use: Treats abdominal cramps, IBS, and gastric spasm
medicines.precautions: Avoid in glaucoma; may cause dry mouth

medicines.name: Rabeprazole
medicines.use: Treats GERD, peptic ulcers, and stomach acid issues
medicines.precautions: Long-term use requires monitoring; may affect magnesium levels

medicines.name: Glimepiride
medicines.use: Lowers blood sugar in Type 2 Diabetes
medicines.precautions: Risk of hypoglycemia; do not skip meals

medicines.name: Sitagliptin
medicines.use: Manages blood sugar in Type 2 Diabetes
medicines.precautions: Adjust dose in kidney disease; rare risk of pancreatitis

medicines.name: Empagliflozin
medicines.use: Lowers blood sugar and has cardiovascular and kidney protective benefits
medicines.precautions: May cause genital infections; maintain hygiene

medicines.name: Clindamycin
medicines.use: Treats skin, bone, and dental bacterial infections
medicines.precautions: Take with full glass of water; report severe diarrhea (C. diff risk)

medicines.name: Tinidazole
medicines.use: Treats bacterial and parasitic gut infections
medicines.precautions: Avoid alcohol during and 72 hrs after treatment

medicines.name: Doxazosin
medicines.use: Treats high blood pressure and urinary symptoms
medicines.precautions: May cause dizziness on standing; start at night

medicines.name: Tamsulosin
medicines.use: Relieves urinary symptoms of enlarged prostate
medicines.precautions: Caution with other blood pressure medicines; may affect ejaculation

medicines.name: Multivitamin Tablet
medicines.use: Provides essential vitamins and minerals for overall health
medicines.precautions: Not a substitute for balanced diet; high-dose supplementation should be avoided

medicines.name: Probiotics (Lactobacillus)
medicines.use: Restores gut flora after antibiotics and treats IBS
medicines.dosage_guidance: One capsule daily with or after meals
medicines.precautions: Refrigerate if required; generally safe; immunocompromised patients should consult doctor

follow_up_questions: How long have you had the fever?
What is your temperature reading?
Do you have a cough or sore throat?
Have you travelled recently?
Are you experiencing chills or night sweats?
general_advice: Stay well hydrated with water and fluids
Rest as much as possible
Use a damp cloth on the forehead to reduce discomfort
Wear light, comfortable clothing
Monitor temperature every few hours
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If fever is above 103°F (39.4°C), lasts more than 3 days, or is accompanied by rash, stiff neck, confusion, or breathing difficulty

follow_up_questions: Where exactly is the pain located?
How severe is the pain on a scale of 1–10?
Is this a new headache or a recurring one?
Are you experiencing nausea or vision changes?
Have you been under stress or sleep-deprived recently?
general_advice: Rest in a quiet, dark room
Stay hydrated and avoid skipping meals
Apply a cold or warm compress to the head
Reduce screen time and manage stress
Avoid loud noises and bright lights
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If headache is sudden and severe ('thunderclap'), associated with high fever, stiff neck, visual loss, or follows a head injury

follow_up_questions: Is it a dry cough or are you bringing up mucus?
How long have you been coughing?
Do you have a fever or sore throat?
Do you smoke or have asthma?
Are you experiencing shortness of breath?
general_advice: Stay hydrated and drink warm fluids
Use honey with warm water to soothe throat
Avoid cold, dry air and irritants like smoke
Try steam inhalation for relief
Rest adequately
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If cough lasts more than 3 weeks, is accompanied by blood, high fever, or severe breathing difficulty

follow_up_questions: How long have you had the sore throat?
Do you have a fever?
Is it painful to swallow?
Do you notice any white patches in your throat?
Are your glands in the neck swollen?
general_advice: Gargle with warm salt water
Drink warm liquids like tea or broth
Avoid cold foods and beverages
Rest your voice and get adequate sleep
Use throat lozenges for temporary relief
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If throat pain is very severe, there are white patches or pus, fever is high, or symptoms last more than 5–7 days

follow_up_questions: Is the discharge clear or coloured (yellow/green)?
Do you also have sneezing and itchy eyes?
How long have you had this symptom?
Any recent exposure to allergens like dust or pollen?
Do you have a fever?
general_advice: Blow nose gently, one nostril at a time
Stay hydrated to thin mucus
Use saline nasal rinse for relief
Avoid known allergens if allergy-related
Rest and keep warm
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If discharge is thick yellow/green and lasts more than 10 days, or is accompanied by facial pain and fever

follow_up_questions: Did the breathing difficulty start suddenly or gradually?
Do you have chest pain or tightness?
Do you have a history of asthma or heart disease?
Are you currently wheezing?
Are you able to lie flat comfortably?
general_advice: Sit upright to ease breathing
Remain calm and breathe slowly and deeply
Use any prescribed inhaler if available
Avoid physical exertion
Ensure fresh air and ventilation
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: Seek immediate medical attention if severe; also consult if breathing difficulty is new, worsening, or associated with chest pain or bluish lips

follow_up_questions: Is the pain sharp, dull, or pressure-like?
Does the pain radiate to your arm, jaw, or back?
Did the pain start with physical activity?
Are you sweating, nauseated, or short of breath?
Do you have a history of heart disease?
general_advice: Stop all physical activity immediately
Sit or lie down in a comfortable position
Do not ignore chest pain
Do not eat or drink until evaluated
Call emergency services if symptoms are severe
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: Seek IMMEDIATE emergency care for any new or unexplained chest pain, especially with sweating, nausea, or pain spreading to arm or jaw

follow_up_questions: How long have you been feeling tired?
Is it affecting your daily activities?
Do you have changes in appetite or weight?
Are you sleeping adequately but still tired?
Do you feel emotionally low or depressed?
general_advice: Ensure 7–9 hours of quality sleep per night
Maintain a balanced, nutritious diet
Stay hydrated throughout the day
Take regular breaks and avoid overexertion
Engage in light physical activity like walking
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If fatigue is severe, persists for more than 2 weeks, or is accompanied by significant weight loss, shortness of breath, or chest pain

follow_up_questions: Is it a spinning sensation (vertigo) or light-headedness?
Does it occur when you stand up quickly?
Are you on any blood pressure medications?
Do you have nausea or vomiting with it?
Have you had any recent head injury?
general_advice: Sit or lie down immediately when dizzy
Drink water slowly
Rise slowly from sitting or lying positions
Avoid sudden head movements
Ensure you are eating regularly
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If dizziness is sudden and severe, recurrent, or accompanied by slurred speech, vision changes, or difficulty walking

follow_up_questions: Did the nausea start after eating?
Have you vomited?
Is there any abdominal pain or cramping?
Have you travelled or eaten outside food recently?
Are you pregnant?
general_advice: Avoid solid food temporarily; sip clear liquids
Eat small, bland meals like toast or rice
Avoid spicy, fatty, or heavy foods
Rest and avoid strong smells
Sip ginger tea for natural relief
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If nausea is persistent for more than 2 days, accompanied by severe abdominal pain, high fever, or blood in vomit

follow_up_questions: How many loose stools per day?
Is there blood or mucus in the stool?
Do you have a fever?
Have you eaten outside or travelled recently?
Are you feeling dizzy or very weak?
general_advice: Drink ORS to stay hydrated
Avoid dairy, spicy, and fatty foods
Eat light foods like bananas, rice, toast
Wash hands frequently
Monitor for signs of dehydration
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If diarrhea contains blood, lasts more than 2 days, or there are signs of severe dehydration (sunken eyes, no urination)

follow_up_questions: Where exactly is the pain located?
Is the pain constant or does it come and go?
Does the pain relate to eating?
Are you also experiencing nausea or vomiting?
Have you had any change in bowel habits?
general_advice: Avoid heavy, spicy, or fatty foods
Eat small, frequent meals
Stay hydrated with plain water
Avoid lying down immediately after eating
Apply a warm compress if cramping
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: Immediately if pain is severe, sudden, or in the lower right abdomen; also if associated with high fever, vomiting, or rigid abdomen

follow_up_questions: How many days since your last bowel movement?
Are stools hard or painful to pass?
How much water and fibre are you consuming?
Are you on any medications?
Is there any bleeding when you pass stool?
general_advice: Increase water intake to at least 2 litres/day
Eat high-fibre foods like fruits, vegetables, and whole grains
Take regular light exercise
Establish a consistent toilet routine
Avoid processed foods and excess dairy
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If constipation lasts more than 2 weeks, is associated with blood in stool, unexplained weight loss, or severe pain

follow_up_questions: Is urination painful or burning?
Are you also excessively thirsty?
Do you have a fever or lower back pain?
Have you noticed any change in urine colour?
Have you been consuming a lot of fluids?
general_advice: Drink sufficient water throughout the day
Avoid caffeine and alcohol which irritate the bladder
Do not hold in urine for extended periods
Maintain good hygiene in genital area
Keep a record of urination frequency
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If accompanied by burning, fever, blood in urine, or if excessive thirst and urination suggest possible diabetes

follow_up_questions: Which joints are affected?
Is there swelling, redness, or warmth in the joint?
Did the pain start after physical activity or injury?
Is the pain worse in the morning?
Do you have a fever along with the joint pain?
general_advice: Rest the affected joint
Apply ice pack for acute pain or swelling
Apply warm compress for stiffness
Perform gentle stretching exercises
Maintain a healthy body weight to reduce joint stress
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If joint is very swollen and hot, if pain is accompanied by high fever and rash, or if multiple joints are involved

follow_up_questions: Is the pain in the upper, middle, or lower back?
Did it start after lifting or physical activity?
Does the pain radiate to your legs?
Do you have any numbness or tingling in legs?
Does lying down relieve or worsen the pain?
general_advice: Apply warm or cold compress to the area
Avoid prolonged sitting or lying in one position
Use a firm mattress for sleep support
Practice good posture at your desk
Perform gentle stretching and core exercises
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If back pain is severe, radiates to legs with numbness, follows an injury, or is accompanied by urinary problems

follow_up_questions: When did the rash appear?
Is it itchy, painful, or neither?
Have you used any new products or eaten new foods?
Is the rash spreading?
Do you have a fever along with the rash?
general_advice: Do not scratch the affected area
Keep skin clean and moisturised
Avoid potential allergens
Wear loose, breathable clothing
Apply calamine lotion for itch relief
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If rash spreads rapidly, is associated with fever and joint pain, involves face/throat, or does not improve in 3–5 days

follow_up_questions: Are you also urinating frequently?
Do you feel unusually tired?
Have you noticed any unintended weight loss?
Is your vision blurry at times?
Do you have a family history of diabetes?
general_advice: Drink water regularly and monitor intake
Avoid sugary drinks and sodas
Eat balanced meals with controlled sugar
Note frequency of thirst and urination to share with doctor
Get blood sugar tested as a precaution
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If excessive thirst is accompanied by frequent urination, fatigue, and blurred vision — get blood sugar checked promptly

follow_up_questions: Is the anxiety constant or triggered by specific situations?
Do you experience physical symptoms like racing heart or sweating?
How is your sleep quality?
Have you had panic attacks?
Have you been under significant stress recently?
general_advice: Practice deep breathing exercises daily
Limit caffeine and alcohol consumption
Maintain a regular sleep routine
Exercise regularly to reduce stress hormones
Talk to a trusted person about your feelings
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If anxiety significantly affects daily functioning, work, or relationships, or if you experience frequent panic attacks

follow_up_questions: Do you have trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking too early?
How many hours of sleep are you getting?
Do you use screens before bed?
Are you under significant stress or anxiety?
Do you consume caffeine or alcohol in the evening?
general_advice: Maintain a consistent sleep and wake time
Avoid screens at least 1 hour before bed
Keep the bedroom cool, dark, and quiet
Avoid caffeine after 2 PM
Try relaxation techniques like deep breathing or meditation
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If insomnia persists for more than 4 weeks, significantly affects daily life, or is associated with depression or severe anxiety

follow_up_questions: How much weight have you lost and over what period?
Was this weight loss intentional?
Do you have changes in appetite?
Do you have night sweats or fever?
Are you experiencing fatigue or weakness?
general_advice: Maintain a food diary to track calorie intake
Eat nutritious, calorie-rich foods
Avoid skipping meals
Consult a nutritionist for diet guidance
Monitor weight weekly
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: Unintentional weight loss of more than 5% of body weight over 6–12 months warrants prompt medical evaluation

follow_up_questions: How often do palpitations occur?
Are they triggered by exercise, stress, or caffeine?
Do you also feel dizzy or short of breath?
Do they start and stop suddenly?
Do you have a history of thyroid or heart disease?
general_advice: Reduce caffeine, tea, and energy drink intake
Practice relaxation techniques
Stay hydrated and maintain regular meals
Avoid smoking and excessive alcohol
Track episodes with timing and triggers
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If palpitations are frequent, associated with chest pain, fainting, or shortness of breath — seek prompt evaluation

follow_up_questions: Is there discharge from the eye?

Is there pain, burning, or itching?
Have you been exposed to someone with pink eye?
Are you wearing contact lenses?

Wash hands frequently
Use clean tissues and do not share towels
Remove contact lenses until symptoms resolve
Clean eyes gently with clean water
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If vision is affected, pain is severe, there is significant discharge, or symptoms do not improve in 3–5 days

follow_up_questions: Is the pain in one or both ears?

Do you have a fever?
Did the pain start after swimming or flying?
Is your hearing muffled?

Apply warm compress to the ear for comfort
Keep ears dry after bathing
Avoid blowing nose forcefully
Rest and take adequate fluids
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If pain is severe, discharge is present, hearing is affected, or fever persists

follow_up_questions: Is it visible to the naked eye or found on a test?
Do you have pain in the lower back or abdomen?
Is there a burning sensation when urinating?
Have you had any recent injury?
Do you have any known kidney or bladder conditions?
general_advice: Stay well hydrated with water
Avoid holding urine for long periods
Do not self-medicate
Note colour and frequency for your doctor
Avoid strenuous physical activity until evaluated
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: Seek prompt medical evaluation for any blood in urine — this always needs investigation to rule out serious causes

follow_up_questions: How often do you experience night sweats?
Do you also have a fever or chills?
Have you noticed unexplained weight loss?
Are you going through menopause (if applicable)?
Are you on any medications?
general_advice: Keep the bedroom cool and well ventilated
Use light, breathable bed linen
Stay hydrated at night
Wear light, moisture-wicking sleepwear
Avoid heavy meals before bedtime
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If night sweats are persistent, associated with fever, weight loss, or swollen lymph nodes — consult a doctor promptly

follow_up_questions: Is the white part of your eyes also yellowing?
Is your urine dark-coloured?
Do you have abdominal pain or itching?
Have you been unwell recently or had contact with someone with hepatitis?
Do you consume alcohol regularly?
general_advice: Avoid alcohol completely
Rest and maintain adequate hydration
Eat light, easily digestible meals
Do not share food, utensils, or personal items
Seek medical attention promptly
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: Seek immediate medical attention for any yellowing of skin or eyes — it always requires medical evaluation

follow_up_questions: Where are the swollen nodes (neck, armpit, groin)?
Are they tender or painful?
Do you have a fever or recent infection?
Have you noticed unexplained weight loss?
Have you had any cuts or injuries nearby?

Stay hydrated and eat nutritious food
Apply a warm compress to reduce tenderness
Avoid self-diagnosis
Monitor for any increase in size
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If nodes are very large, hard, painless, or persist for more than 2 weeks, or if unexplained weight loss and night sweats are present

follow_up_questions: Did the pain start after physical activity?
Is it localised to one area or widespread?
Do you have fever with muscle pain?
Have you recently started new exercise?
Is the pain constant or does it improve with rest?
general_advice: Rest and avoid strenuous activity
Apply warm or cold compress
Stay hydrated
Stretch gently after rest
Ensure adequate Vitamin D and calcium intake
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If muscle pain is severe, widespread, associated with dark urine or significant weakness, or follows high fever

follow_up_questions: When does heartburn typically occur — after meals or at night?
Do you feel a burning sensation rising in your chest?
What foods tend to trigger it?
Do you lie down shortly after eating?
Have you noticed any difficulty swallowing?
general_advice: Eat smaller, more frequent meals
Avoid spicy, fatty, and acidic foods
Do not lie down for at least 2 hours after eating
Elevate the head of your bed slightly
Avoid tight clothing around the abdomen
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If heartburn occurs more than twice a week, causes difficulty swallowing, or is associated with weight loss or vomiting

follow_up_questions: How long have you had reduced appetite?
Have you lost weight as a result?
Do you have nausea or abdominal pain?
Are you feeling sad or low in mood?
Are you on any new medications?
general_advice: Eat small amounts every 2–3 hours even if not hungry
Choose nutrient-dense foods
Avoid skipping meals entirely
Identify and address underlying stress
Stay hydrated with soups and fluids
guidance_mapping.when_to_consult_doctor: If appetite loss persists for more than a week, is associated with significant weight loss, or accompanied by jaundice, high fever, or persistent nausea
